title: Materials Basics

video.note: Covers material example setup, starter materials, creating a material, the material graph, Base Color, Roughness, Metallic, PBR, Apply/Save, and simple texture-driven variation.

learningTarget: I can create a basic Unreal material and control how it looks using base color, roughness, metallic, and simple texture-driven variation.

bellRinger.prompt: How can you tell whether a surface is metal, plastic, stone, wood, or paint just by looking at it?

vocabulary.definition: An Unreal asset that defines how a surface looks when rendered.
The node-based workspace used to build and edit a material.
A block in the graph that stores or processes information before sending it to a material input.
The main visible color of a material before lighting effects are applied.
A value that controls how sharp or blurry reflections appear on a surface.
A value that controls whether a surface behaves like metal or a non-metal.
Physically based rendering, a workflow that uses realistic material values such as metallic and roughness.
A single number value, often used for settings such as Roughness or Metallic.
A multi-number value often used to represent RGB color.
The material editor action that updates the material so changes appear in the level.

assignment.title: First Custom Material
evidenceRequired: Screenshot of the material applied to an object.
Short reflection explaining base color, roughness, and metallic.

exitTicket: What happens if you change a material but forget to press Apply?
